1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")

11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18

20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25

27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

   - Arguments: `id` (required)

- Columns: ID (8 chars), Title (30 chars), URL (40 chars), Tags (20 chars)

- Returns separator row + header + data rows
- Returns "No bookmarks found." for empty list

- Pretty-prints with 2-space indentation

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

The `__init__.py` is empty. Now I have a complete understanding of the codebase. Here is the detailed implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

### Overview

Two files need to be changed: one new file (`src/search.py`) must be created, and one existing file (`src/cli.py`) must be modified. The changes are minimal and follow the established project conventions precisely.

---

### Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

This is the core new module. It must export exactly four functions matching the test imports:

```python
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title
```

**File: `src/search.py`**

The module needs these imports:
- `from urllib.parse import urlparse` -- for extracting the hostname from URLs
- `from typing import List, Optional` -- for type annotations
- `from .models import Bookmark` -- for the Bookmark type

#### Function 1: `search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`

Logic: Case-insensitive substring match on `bookmark.title`. Return all bookmarks where `query.lower()` is found in `bookmark.title.lower()`.

Test-derived behavior:
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")` must match "Python Tutorial" (aaa-111) and "RunPod Python SDK" (bbb-222) -- confirming case-insensitive substring.
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")` must match only "argparse documentation" (eee-555) -- confirming substring, not whole-word.
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")` must return `[]`.

Implementation:
```python
def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    query_lower = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if query_lower in b.title.lower()]
```

#### Function 2: `search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`

Logic: Exact tag match -- `tag` must appear in `bookmark.tags` list.

Test-derived behavior:
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")` returns 1 result (ddd-444).
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")` returns 3 results (aaa-111, ccc-333, eee-555).
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, "rust")` returns `[]`.

Implementation:
```python
def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]
```

#### Function 3: `search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`

Logic: Extract hostname from `bookmark.url` using `urlparse().netloc` and check if it matches the provided domain string.

Test-derived behavior:
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")` returns 2 results (bbb-222 with `github.com/runpod/runpod-python`, ddd-444 with `github.com/pallets/flask`).
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")` returns 2 results (aaa-111, eee-555).
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "stackoverflow.com")` returns `[]`.

The `urlparse` approach will work here because:
- `urlparse("https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html").netloc` returns `"docs.python.org"` -- exact match.
- `urlparse("https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python").netloc` returns `"github.com"` -- exact match.

Implementation:
```python
def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]
```

#### Function 4: `search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title: Optional[str] = None, tag: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Bookmark]`

Logic: AND logic across all provided criteria. When no criteria are given, return all bookmarks. Each non-None criterion filters down the result set.

Test-derived behavior:
- `search(bookmarks, title="flask")` -- single criterion, 1 result.
- `search(bookmarks, tag="javascript")` -- single criterion, 1 result.
- `search(bookmarks, domain="developer.mozilla.org")` -- single criterion, 1 result.
- `search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")` -- AND logic, only bbb-222 has both "python" in title and "sdk" tag.
- `search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="docs", domain="docs.python.org")` -- all three, only aaa-111 matches.
- `search(bookmarks)` -- no criteria, returns all 5.
- `search(bookmarks, title="flask", tag="javascript")` -- conflicting, returns `[]`.

Implementation approach -- successively filter using the individual search functions:
```python
def search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title: Optional[str] = None, tag: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Bookmark]:
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
```

This chaining approach naturally implements AND logic: each filter narrows the set further. When no filters are specified, the original list is returned unchanged.

---

### Step 2: Modify `src/cli.py`

Two changes are needed in this file:

#### Change A: Add import for the search function

Add to the existing imports at the top of the file (line 4-5 area):

```python
from .search import search
```

This follows the existing pattern of importing from sibling modules (e.g., `from .storage import ...`, `from .formatter import ...`).

#### Change B: Add `search` subparser in `build_parser()`

Insert a new subparser block after the existing "show" subparser (after line 39, before the `return parser` on line 41). Follow the exact same pattern used by the "list" subparser:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (substring match)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact match)")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

The tests verify:
- `parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])` produces `args.command == "search"` and `args.title == "python"`.
- `parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])` produces `args.command == "search"`, `args.tag == "docs"`, `args.format == "json"`.

The `--format` argument must use `choices=["table", "json"]` and `default="table"`, matching the list command exactly.

#### Change C: Add command handler in `main()`

Add an `elif` branch for the "search" command after the existing "show" handler (after line 81). This follows the same output pattern as the "list" command:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

This mirrors the "list" command handler at lines 61-66 but passes the loaded bookmarks through `search()` first.

---

### Step 3: Verification

Run the full test suite to confirm:
1. All 18 tests in `test_search.py` pass.
2. All existing tests in `test_storage.py` continue to pass.

Command: `python -m pytest tests/ -v`

---

### Design Decisions and Rationale

1. **`urllib.parse.urlparse` for domain extraction**: This is the standard library approach. `urlparse(url).netloc` cleanly extracts the hostname (including subdomain) from any well-formed URL. No third-party dependencies needed. The test data all uses `https://` scheme URLs, so `netloc` will always be populated.

2. **Chaining filters in `search()` rather than combining predicates**: Chaining `search_by_*` calls is more readable, reuses the individual functions, and naturally implements AND logic. The slight inefficiency of iterating the list up to three times is negligible for a local bookmark manager.

3. **`default=None` for search arguments**: Using `None` as the default for `--title`, `--tag`, and `--domain` allows the `search()` function to distinguish "not provided" from "empty string", which aligns with the test expectation that `search(bookmarks)` (no criteria) returns all bookmarks.

4. **No changes to `models.py`, `storage.py`, or `formatter.py`**: The search module operates on in-memory `List[Bookmark]` objects. It does not need storage access -- that responsibility stays in the CLI handler which calls `load_bookmarks()` first. The formatter is reused unchanged.

5. **Tag matching is exact, not case-insensitive**: The tests use lowercase tags throughout and check for exact membership (`tag in bookmark.tags`). There is no test case for case-insensitive tag matching, so simple `in` comparison is correct.

---

### Potential Pitfalls

- **The `--format` argument on the search subparser**: Both `list` and `search` subparsers define their own `--format` argument. This is fine because argparse scopes arguments to subparsers. However, it is essential that `default="table"` is set, because the test `test_search_command_exists` parses `["search", "--title", "python"]` without `--format` and will fail if `args.format` is undefined when the search handler tries to access it.

- **Import ordering**: The test file uses `from src.search import ...` (not `from .search`), relying on the package being installed or the project root being in `sys.path`. The `pyproject.toml` already configures `src` as the package, so this should work with the existing test setup.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383015280-bymxt3/src/search.py` (new file to create)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383015280-bymxt3/src/cli.py` (add search subparser and handler)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383015280-bymxt3/tests/test_search.py` (the test specification -- read-only reference)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383015280-bymxt3/src/models.py` (Bookmark dataclass -- read-only reference)

## Implementation Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

### Overview

Two files need to be changed: one new file (`src/search.py`) must be created, and one existing file (`src/cli.py`) must be modified. The changes are minimal and follow the established project conventions precisely.

---

### Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

This is the core new module. It must export exactly four functions matching the test imports:

```python
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title
```

**File: `src/search.py`**

The module needs these imports:
- `from urllib.parse import urlparse` -- for extracting the hostname from URLs
- `from typing import List, Optional` -- for type annotations
- `from .models import Bookmark` -- for the Bookmark type

#### Function 1: `search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`

Logic: Case-insensitive substring match on `bookmark.title`. Return all bookmarks where `query.lower()` is found in `bookmark.title.lower()`.

Test-derived behavior:
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")` must match "Python Tutorial" (aaa-111) and "RunPod Python SDK" (bbb-222) -- confirming case-insensitive substring.
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")` must match only "argparse documentation" (eee-555) -- confirming substring, not whole-word.
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")` must return `[]`.

Implementation:
```python
def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    query_lower = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if query_lower in b.title.lower()]
```

#### Function 2: `search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`

Logic: Exact tag match -- `tag` must appear in `bookmark.tags` list.

Test-derived behavior:
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")` returns 1 result (ddd-444).
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")` returns 3 results (aaa-111, ccc-333, eee-555).
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, "rust")` returns `[]`.

Implementation:
```python
def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]
```

#### Function 3: `search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`

Logic: Extract hostname from `bookmark.url` using `urlparse().netloc` and check if it matches the provided domain string.

Test-derived behavior:
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")` returns 2 results (bbb-222 with `github.com/runpod/runpod-python`, ddd-444 with `github.com/pallets/flask`).
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")` returns 2 results (aaa-111, eee-555).
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "stackoverflow.com")` returns `[]`.

The `urlparse` approach will work here because:
- `urlparse("https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html").netloc` returns `"docs.python.org"` -- exact match.
- `urlparse("https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python").netloc` returns `"github.com"` -- exact match.

Implementation:
```python
def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]
```

#### Function 4: `search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title: Optional[str] = None, tag: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Bookmark]`

Logic: AND logic across all provided criteria. When no criteria are given, return all bookmarks. Each non-None criterion filters down the result set.

Test-derived behavior:
- `search(bookmarks, title="flask")` -- single criterion, 1 result.
- `search(bookmarks, tag="javascript")` -- single criterion, 1 result.
- `search(bookmarks, domain="developer.mozilla.org")` -- single criterion, 1 result.
- `search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")` -- AND logic, only bbb-222 has both "python" in title and "sdk" tag.
- `search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="docs", domain="docs.python.org")` -- all three, only aaa-111 matches.
- `search(bookmarks)` -- no criteria, returns all 5.
- `search(bookmarks, title="flask", tag="javascript")` -- conflicting, returns `[]`.

```python
def search(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], title: Optional[str] = None, tag: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Bookmark]:

    if title is not None:

    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
```

This chaining approach naturally implements AND logic: each filter narrows the set further. When no filters are specified, the original list is returned unchanged.

- `parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])` produces `args.command == "search"`, `args.tag == "docs"`, `args.format == "json"`.

The `--format` argument must use `choices=["table", "json"]` and `default="table"`, matching the list command exactly.

### Design Decisions and Rationale

1. **`urllib.parse.urlparse` for domain extraction**: This is the standard library approach. `urlparse(url).netloc` cleanly extracts the hostname (including subdomain) from any well-formed URL. Create a new module with 4 functions. Uses only stdlib (`urllib.parse`) and the existing `Bookmark` model.

- Returns filtered list

- Returns filtered list

### `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Extract hostname via `urlparse(b.url).netloc`, compare with `== domain`
- Returns filtered list

### `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Chain the individual search functions for AND logic
- Each non-None parameter narrows the result set by calling the corresponding `search_by_*` function
- No criteria provided -> return all bookmarks unchanged

## Step 2: Modify `src/cli.py`

### 2a: Add import (top of file, after existing imports)
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b: Add search subparser in `build_parser()` (after the `show` subparser, before `return parser` on line 41)
```python

search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact match)")

    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

Follows the exact same pattern as the existing `list` command handler (lines 61-66).

- **`urllib.parse.urlparse` for domain extraction**: stdlib, no new deps. `urlparse(url).netloc` correctly extracts hostnames like `docs.python.org` and `github.com` from the test URLs.

```bash

```

27	        return cls(
28	            id=data["id"],
29	            url=data["url"],
30	            title=data["title"],
31	            tags=data.get("tags", []),
32	            notes=data.get("notes", ""),

1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
5	from .formatter import format_table, format_json
6	
7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

3	
4	from .models import Bookmark

6	
7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

"""Tests for search functionality.

These tests define the expected behaviour of the search module that needs
to be implemented in src/search.py and wired into the CLI.
"""

import json
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title


@pytest.fixture
def bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html",
            title="Python Tutorial",
            tags=["python", "tutorial", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python tutorial",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python",
            title="RunPod Python SDK",
            tags=["python", "sdk", "runpod"],
            notes="RunPod serverless SDK",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ccc-333",
            url="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript",
            title="MDN JavaScript Guide",
            tags=["javascript", "docs", "mdn"],
            notes="Comprehensive JS reference",
            created_at="2026-01-03T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ddd-444",
            url="https://github.com/pallets/flask",
            title="Flask Web Framework",
            tags=["python", "flask", "web"],
            notes="Lightweight WSGI web framework",
            created_at="2026-01-04T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="eee-555",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html",
            title="argparse documentation",
            tags=["python", "cli", "docs"],
            notes="Argparse module reference",
            created_at="2026-01-05T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


# ---------- search_by_title ----------


class TestSearchByTitle:
    def test_case_insensitive_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")
        titles = [b.title for b in result]
        assert "Python Tutorial" in titles
        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "eee-555"

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_tag ----------


class TestSearchByTag:
    def test_exact_tag_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_shared_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

"""Search functionality for bookmarks."""

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains *query* (case-insensitive)."""
    query_lower = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if query_lower in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact match for *tag*."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL hostname matches *domain*."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).hostname == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Search bookmarks by combining criteria with AND logic.

    When no criteria are provided, all bookmarks are returned.
    """
    results = list(bookmarks)
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results

import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json
from .search import search


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    # --- search ---
    search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
    search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (case-insensitive substring)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Search by exact tag")
    search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Search by URL domain")
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
